title: chrysanthemum parthenium
intro: Chrysanthemum parthenium, also known as Feverfew, is a flowering plant in the daisy family with daisy-like flowers that are typically white with yellow centers. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Feverfew include reducing fever, relieving headaches and migraines, and alleviating symptoms of allergies and digestive issues, such as nausea and cramps. Feverfew can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, capsules, and tinctures for medicinal use, and it is also used in homeopathic remedies.
